_doc: Same baseline format as v5_fixtures/baselines.json. Listed fixtures are expected to produce up to N errors. These are direct ports of upstream svelte2tsx fixtures that test verbatim-emit behavior — preserving user code character-for-character even when the user's code references undefined names, unresolved modules, or other things TS would flag.

$store-as-directive.reason: Tests store-name-as-directive emit shape; user code has TS oddities (unused comma operator side) that svelte2tsx preserves verbatim.

$store-assign.reason: User code references `writable` without importing it. Verbatim emit preserves the unresolved name.

$store-inside-block-without-braces.reason: Multiple `$store` references where only some `store` names are declared in the fixture; verbatim emit preserves the unresolved ones.

$store-nested-declaration.reason: Implicit-any in JSDoc-typed JS under strict mode. Verbatim emit doesn't add type annotations.

$store-no-instance-only-module-script.reason: import from './somewhere' that doesn't exist. Verbatim emit preserves the unresolved import.

store-destructuring.reason: User code references `fromSomewhere()` without import. Verbatim emit preserves the unresolved name.

store-from-module.reason: import from './store' that doesn't exist. Verbatim emit preserves.

store-import.reason: import from './store' that doesn't exist. Verbatim emit preserves.

store-property-access.reason: User code references `someStore` without declaration. Verbatim emit preserves.

uses-$store.reason: User code references `$b` where `b` is never declared (external store assumed). Verbatim emit preserves.
